sentence: Chapter~3 mapped memory hierarchy and legality constraints across GPU, CPU, and NPU classes; this chapter applies that map to MLIR with the same problem-first discipline as Chapter~1 .

sentence: The mistake to avoid is treating compiler or kernel work as a single-hardware recipe---every section below states what breaks on decode fleets, which hardware tier caps the fix, and how passes or megakernels recover bytes/token and launches/token on the SKU you ship .
title: An MLIR-Based Compiler for Hardware Acceleration with Recursion Support: (PhD Forum Paper)

sentence: Teams adopt MLIR because it promises one IR for ``all targets''---then discover that decode SLOs still diverge by an order of magnitude across GPU, CPU, and NPU fleets running the same high-level graph .
title: SPNC: An Open-Source MLIR-Based Compiler for Fast Sum-Product Network Inference on CPUs and GPUs

sentence: The failure mode is not missing dialects; it is treating MLIR as target-neutral syntax instead of a contract layer that must encode residency, launch granularity, and static legality from Chapter~2--3 .
title: LAGrad: Statically Optimized Differentiable Programming in MLIR

sentence: This chapter maps MLIR's hardware support matrix, pass pipelines, codegen backends, and autotuning pitfalls to LLM decode metrics---bytes/token and launches/token---not peak compile-time FLOPs .
title: An MLIR-Based Compiler for Hardware Acceleration with Recursion Support: (PhD Forum Paper)

sentence: Unlike monolithic IRs that hard-code one target, MLIR composes dialects---Linalg for math, memref for buffers, scf for structured control flow, gpu for launches---and connects them with explicit conversions .

sentence: Production incidents often blame ``the NVVM backend,'' when the real bug was emitting LLVM IR that assumes a mutable stack and per-op malloc in the host wrapper---poison for CUDA Graphs capture and for NPU static schedules alike .
title: CUDA Graphs

sentence: Codegen is where MLIR's target abstraction meets launch reality: GPU emit paths must respect stream ordering, cluster scope, and TMA descriptor lifetimes; CPU emit must preserve NUMA-local buffers; NPU platform emit must serialize DMA chains with compile-time-known latencies .
title: SPNC: An Open-Source MLIR-Based Compiler for Fast Sum-Product Network Inference on CPUs and GPUs

sentence: NVVM/ROCDL paths excel when decode graphs contain dynamic control flow that still maps to SIMT; bare-metal AIE paths excel when the entire decoder step is a static dataflow graph with no host orchestration inside the step .

title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: Treat MLIR like a hardware dependency with qualification suites, not a semver library .
title: An MLIR-Based Compiler for Hardware Acceleration with Recursion Support: (PhD Forum Paper)

sentence: MLIR is often the staging IR for MegaKernel fusion described in Part~IV .
title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: The fusion boundary is not ``all layers in one kernel'' for every target---GPU may fuse attention+MLP; NPU may require whole-decoder static graphs; CPU may fuse norm+GEMM only .
title: Ednet: Edge-optimized small target detection in UAV imagery-faster context attention, better feature fusion, and hardware acceleration

sentence: MLIR passes must encode those boundaries as explicit attributes so codegen does not over-fuse into illegal register files or under-fuse into launch storms .
title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: YiRage exports fusion contracts from MLIR into backend-specific MegaKernel templates rather than relying on a single linalg-fuse preset .
title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

sentence: Unit-test individual MLIR passes on toy matmuls; integration-test full decoder exports on three targets; fleet-test with decode traces and graph capture .
title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: Skipping the middle layer---full decoder on GPU+CPU+NPU---is how teams ship passes that optimize square GEMMs but break paged KV .
title: SPNC: An Open-Source MLIR-Based Compiler for Fast Sum-Product Network Inference on CPUs and GPUs

sentence: The testing pyramid mirrors Chapter~3's benchmark methodology: metrics-first, hardware-labeled, trace-driven .
title: Ednet: Edge-optimized small target detection in UAV imagery-faster context attention, better feature fusion, and hardware acceleration

sentence: When decode latency regresses after an MLIR upgrade, capture IR dumps at three anchors: post-import, post-bufferization, post-lowering .
title: Finding Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure via Lowering Space Exploration

sentence: Diff allocators and gpu.launch counts first---they correlate with TaxBreak launch metrics faster than LLVM IR line counts .
title: SPNC: An Open-Source MLIR-Based Compiler for Fast Sum-Product Network Inference on CPUs and GPUs

sentence: For NPU incidents, diff DMA edge counts and static shape flags before reading assembly .
title: LAGrad: Statically Optimized Differentiable Programming in MLIR

sentence: YiRage stores these dumps keyed by model hash and shape bucket so on-call engineers can bisect pass regressions without reproducing full training stacks .
title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

sentence: MLIR's value for LLM decode is not ``write once, run everywhere.'' It is ``analyze once, lower with target-specific contracts.'' The hardware matrix (Section~ sec:ch14_mlir_hw_matrix) must gate dynamicism and residency before canonicalization .
title: Ednet: Edge-optimized small target detection in UAV imagery-faster context attention, better feature fusion, and hardware acceleration

sentence: Pass pipelines (Section~ sec:ch14_mlir_arch_passes) must declare which dimension they optimize---SRAM, launches, or bytes---and fork early per target .
title: LAGrad: Statically Optimized Differentiable Programming in MLIR

sentence: Codegen (Section~ sec:ch14_mlir_codegen) must produce capturable GPU wrappers and static NPU DMA schedules, not pretty LLVM .
title: SPNC: An Open-Source MLIR-Based Compiler for Fast Sum-Product Network Inference on CPUs and GPUs

sentence: Autotuning (Section~ sec:ch14_mlir_tuning) must optimize decode traces with multi-objective gates, not square GEMM winners .
title: LAGrad: Statically Optimized Differentiable Programming in MLIR

sentence: Case studies (Section~ sec:ch14_mlir_case_study) must report legality failures and launches/token alongside latency .
title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: MLIR is the right infrastructure for multi-hardware LLM compilers only when dialects, passes, codegen, and autotuning are constrained by explicit target matrices and decode metrics .
title: An MLIR-Based Compiler for Hardware Acceleration with Recursion Support: (PhD Forum Paper)

sentence: Treating MLIR as target-agnostic IR reproduces the framework fragmentation Chapter~1 diagnosed---now with prettier compiler logs .
title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: Carry forward: (1) predicate every pass on residency and launch count; (2) fork emit paths early; (3) benchmark bytes/token and launches/token on GPU, CPU, and NPU before declaring victory .
title: SPNC: An Open-Source MLIR-Based Compiler for Fast Sum-Product Network Inference on CPUs and GPUs

sentence: Avoid MLIR chapters that list dialect names without decode metrics .
title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: This book's MLIR chapter is intentionally metric-anchored because ``modern compiler infrastructure'' alone does not explain why MoE decode still hits seconds-per-token on unfused stacks .
title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: Subsequent chapters cover stacks that consume or embed MLIR .
title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: XLA brings its own HLO world; TVM brings schedule languages; Triton sits closer to tile programming .

sentence: The evaluation discipline---bytes/token, launches/token, legality per target---remains constant even when the surface syntax changes .

sentence: Teams should not re-learn metrics per compiler; they should re-learn predicates and pass boundaries .

sentence: Part~VI continues with production-grade stacks (XLA, TVM, Triton) that sit above or beside MLIR; the evaluation discipline remains identical .
title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: MLIR wins when those numbers move; infrastructure alone is insufficient .
title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: MLIR HW matrix exposes a cross-hardware fracture: the same fused subgraph legal on Hopper can be rejected outright on XDNA or CPU cache budgets.
title: An MLIR-Based Compiler for Hardware Acceleration with Recursion Support: (PhD Forum Paper)

sentence: Binding software to silicon: compiler passes must encode hardware/target legality before codegen, not as comments in hand-written kernels .
title: Ednet: Edge-optimized small target detection in UAV imagery-faster context attention, better feature fusion, and hardware acceleration

sentence: YiRage runs target-aware checks against chip models from Chapter~3; manual stacks should treat failed legality as a release blocker, not a backend TODO.
title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

sentence: When MLIR HW matrix disagrees with microbenchmarks, trust fleet telemetry: fix orchestration and tier placement first, then revisit MMA tile shapes .
title: MLIR-to-CGRA: A Versatile MLIR-Based Compiler Framework for CGRAs

sentence: Before merging compiler changes affecting MLIR HW matrix, attach roofline or NPU buffer accounting showing hardware residency fits on-chip for the target SKU .
title: An MLIR-Based Compiler for Hardware Acceleration with Recursion Support: (PhD Forum Paper)

sentence: A Hopper decode cell may legalize hardware with TMA-backed double buffering while an XDNA cell requires the same math expressed as static tile assignments---identical FLOPs, different bytes/token because DDR traffic differs by an order of magnitude .
title: Hardware-in-the-loop implementation of projectile target search algorithm for selective harmonic elimination in a 3-phase multilevel converter

sentence: The usual MLIR failure at MLIR arch passes is importing a CUDA mental model and expecting runtime scheduling to hide illegal buffer lifetimes.
title: DESIL: Detecting Silent Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure

sentence: On GPUs that means shared-memory/TMA budgets and warp-grain barriers; on XDNA/AIE it means static tile buffers, DMA chains, and carrier slots with compile-time lifetimes; on CPUs it means L2/L3 blocking and NUMA-local KV placement .
title: SPNC: An Open-Source MLIR-Based Compiler for Fast Sum-Product Network Inference on CPUs and GPUs

sentence: Decode at batch-1 keeps arithmetic intensity on the memory-bandwidth slope---micro-optimizing isolated GEMMs rarely moves p99 latency.
title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

sentence: Compiler takeaway for MLIR arch passes: lower the same decoder IR, but predicate passes on target residency tables---never ship a GPU-only schedule to NPU fleets .
title: Finding Bugs in MLIR Compiler Infrastructure via Lowering Space Exploration

sentence: Success metrics: bytes/token, launches/token, and p99 decode latency; compare GPU, CPU, and NPU cells on identical context lengths.
title: SPNC: An Open-Source MLIR-Based Compiler for Fast Sum-Product Network Inference on CPUs and GPUs

sentence: A Hopper decode cell may legalize pass with TMA-backed double buffering while an XDNA cell requires the same math expressed as static tile assignments---identical FLOPs, different bytes/token because DDR traffic differs by an order of magnitude .

sentence: Teams that A/B only prefill confuse the story: lower constraints bite hardest at batch-1 decode where launches/token and KV read bandwidth dominate .
